title: Immigrant/Refugee Employment Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Immigrant/Refugee Employment Programs 7 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ACCENT NETWORK IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support with resumes, job searches, credential evaluation, and digital literacy, along with career counseling and career planning guidance. Also offers financial wellness services, including workshops on saving, budgeting, and debt management, and one-on-one coaching. 12254 SW Garden Place Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers with a focus on Russian and Ukrainian population Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-8pm Email (971) 280-6738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides social services case management for asylum seekers, refugees, and others with immigration status for help with food, transportation, health services, OHP (Oregon Health Plan), RCA (Refugee Cash Assistance), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ), TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families ), WIC (Women, Infants and Children), and other services. Also provides employment and legal services. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies according to program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 1326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides social services case management for asylum seekers, refugees, and others with immigration status for help with food, transportation, health services, OHP (Oregon Health Plan), RCA (Refugee Cash Assistance), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ), TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families ), WIC (Women, Infants and Children), and other services. Also provides employment and legal services. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies according to program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 1326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Employment and training services for newly arrived refugees and established refugees and immigrants, including job coaching, placement, retention, and career planning. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Refugees and immigrants Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6461 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO EASTERN OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, resource referral, and wraparound services for refugee families. Programming includes refugee employment services, ESL classes, and youth refugee mentorship. 723 S Oregon Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 222-5391 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE SE WORKS FOSTER PROFESSIONAL IMMIGRANT CREDENTIAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps immigrants and refugees find long-term jobs based on the degrees or certificates they earned in their home countries. Provides a career coach to help with credential recognition, creating an employment plan, understanding American workplace culture, and ongoing job placement support. Also offers education and skills training through on-the-job training, paid internships, and workshops. 6401 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Immigrants with degrees or certifications from a country that is not the United States of America Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 772-2330 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE SE WORKS HAROLD PROFESSIONAL IMMIGRANT CREDENTIAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps immigrants and refugees find long-term jobs based on the degrees or certificates they earned in their home countries. Provides a career coach to help with credential recognition, creating an employment plan, understanding American workplace culture, and ongoing job placement support. Also offers education and skills training through on-the-job training, paid internships, and workshops. 8044 SE Harold Street Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Immigrants with degrees or certifications from a country that is not the United States of America Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 772-2330 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCENT NETWORK IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION EMPLOYMENT IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO EASTERN OREGON SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE PROFESSIONAL IMMIGRANT CREDENTIAL PROGRAM SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE PROFESSIONAL IMMIGRANT CREDENTIAL PROGRAM
